subject: urllib.request.urlopen allows file:// scheme by default
truth: Stop using urllib.request.urlopen without restricting schemes; use requests library or validate the URL scheme against an allowlist.
quote: Audit url open for permitted schemes. Allowing use of 'file:' or custom schemes is often unexpected.

subject: urllib.request.urlretrieve allows file:// scheme by default
truth: Stop using urllib.request.urlretrieve without restricting schemes; it can write local files from file:// URLs.
quote: Audit url open for permitted schemes. Allowing use of 'file:' or custom schemes is often unexpected.

subject: urllib.request.URLopener and FancyURLopener allow file:// scheme by default
truth: Stop using urllib.request.URLopener or FancyURLopener without restricting schemes; they open file:// URLs by default.
quote: Audit url open for permitted schemes. Allowing use of 'file:' or custom schemes is often unexpected.

subject: six.moves.urllib.request functions allow file:// scheme by default
truth: Stop using six.moves.urllib.request.urlopen/urlretrieve/URLopener without restricting schemes; they inherit the same file:// vulnerability.
quote: Audit url open for permitted schemes. Allowing use of 'file:' or custom schemes is often unexpected.

subject: FTP_TLS without SSL context
truth: When using FTP_TLS, always pass ssl.create_default_context() to the context parameter; do not call FTP_TLS without explicit SSL context.

subject: SQL injection via string concatenation
truth: Stop concatenating user input directly into SQL query strings; use parameterized queries with placeholders instead.
quote: concatenating user input directly into SQL query strings
code_bad:
query := "SELECT * FROM users WHERE name = '" + name + "'"
db.Query(query)
code_good: db.Query("SELECT * FROM users WHERE name = ?", name)

subject: SQL injection via tainted struct field
truth: Stop passing user-controlled struct fields as raw SQL queries; use parameterized queries even when input flows through structs or closures.
quote: user-controlled struct fields as raw SQL queries
code_good: db.Query("SELECT * FROM users WHERE name = ?", name)

subject: SQL injection via fmt.Sprintf
truth: Stop using fmt.Sprintf to build SQL queries with user input; use parameterized queries with ? placeholders.
quote: fmt.Sprintf to build SQL queries with user input
code_bad:
query := fmt.Sprintf("DELETE FROM users WHERE id = %s", id)
db.Exec(query)
code_good: db.Query("SELECT * FROM users WHERE name = ?", name)
